title: Semiconductor Equipment Market Size to Hit USD 329.73 Billion by 2035 | Research by SNS Insider
author: Sns Insider
description: The global semiconductor equipment market is valued at $138.64 billion in 2025 and is expected to grow to $329.73 billion by 2035, with a CAGR of 9.05%. Growth is driven by rising AI and 5G adoption, government-backed fab investments, and increasing demand across consumer electronics, automotive, and telecommunications sectors. Wafer fabrication equipment dominates the market, while testing equipment and inspection & metrology segments show the fastest growth rates.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: ASML is highlighted for expanding EUV lithography system capabilities in October 2025, supporting advanced node semiconductor manufacturing and enhancing chip performance across leading foundries and IDMs, positioning it well for market growth.
Listed as a leading market player in semiconductor equipment manufacturing, positioned to benefit from the projected 9.05% CAGR growth in the global semiconductor equipment market through 2035.
Listed as a leading market player in semiconductor equipment, well-positioned to capitalize on growing demand for wafer fabrication equipment and advanced manufacturing technologies.

Listed as a leading market player, positioned to benefit from fastest-growing inspection & metrology segment driven by increasing precision requirements in next-generation semiconductor manufacturing.

Tokyo Electron introduced advanced etching and deposition equipment in August 2025 designed for next-generation semiconductor manufacturing and sub-3nm node production, demonstrating innovation aligned with market demand.

title: Has the Next Leg of the AI Trade Arrived?
author: David Scutt
description: Chinese tech stocks surged on reports that Tencent is launching a WeChat AI assistant, suggesting the market may be shifting focus from AI infrastructure to AI adoption. Tencent, Alibaba, and Meituan led gains while Asian semiconductor stocks weakened, indicating a potential rotation in AI investment strategy.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock rose 7.8% on reports of launching a WeChat AI assistant, demonstrating practical AI deployment across a major digital ecosystem
Gained 5.7% as part of the broader Chinese tech rally driven by AI adoption prospects
Climbed 8.8% alongside other Chinese tech stocks benefiting from AI adoption narrative
Added 3.3% as Chinese tech stocks rallied on AI adoption developments
Slipped as Asian semiconductor stocks faced weakness during the market rotation away from AI infrastructure
Declined as part of the broader semiconductor weakness amid shifting AI investment focus

title: Global Semiconductor Etch Equipment Market Poised for Strong Growth as Rising Chip Demand and Advanced Fabrication Technologies Accelerate Adoption: Verified Market Research®
author: Verified Market Research®
description: The global semiconductor etch equipment market is projected to grow at a CAGR of 8.7% from 2026 to 2032, reaching USD 28.26 billion by 2032 from USD 14.51 billion in 2024. Growth is driven by advanced semiconductor node manufacturing, AI/IoT demand, and global fab capacity expansion. However, high capital costs, supply chain disruptions, and export control constraints remain key challenges.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Listed as a key player in the semiconductor etch equipment market, which is experiencing strong growth driven by advanced node manufacturing and increased fab investments globally.
Identified as a major competitor in the growing semiconductor etch equipment market with sustained demand from foundries and IDMs scaling investments in advanced fabrication technologies.
Key player in the market benefiting from the 8.7% CAGR growth and strong demand in Asia-Pacific region, particularly from Taiwan and South Korea's advanced node production.

title: Rapid Thermal Processing (RTP) Equipment Market Size to Hit USD 3.90 Billion by 2035 | SNS Insider
author: Sns Insider
description: The U.S. Rapid Thermal Processing (RTP) Equipment Market is projected to grow at a CAGR of 12.08% from 2026 to 2035, driven by increasing semiconductor fabrication, advanced node chip production, and rising demand for AI and 5G chips. The global RTP market is valued at USD 1.77 billion in 2025 and expected to reach USD 3.90 billion by 2035 with a CAGR of 8.27%. Rapid Thermal Annealing (RTA) dominates with 42% market share, while 300mm wafers lead with 64% share. Asia-Pacific holds 50.2% of the global market, with North America growing fastest at 12.60% CAGR.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company introduced advanced RTP solutions in July 2024 designed to improve wafer annealing precision and support next-generation semiconductor nodes for AI and 5G, positioning it well in a growing market.
Listed as a leading market player but no specific recent developments or market activity mentioned in the article.

Launched next-generation thermal processing equipment in September 2024 aimed at improving temperature uniformity and process control for advanced semiconductor nodes, demonstrating active innovation in a high-growth market.

title: The Memory Shortage Trade: 7 Stocks Goldman Says Can Keep Running
author: Piero Cingari
description: Goldman Sachs predicts a severe global memory chip shortage through 2027, with DRAM and NAND undersupply reaching 15+ year highs. This is expected to drive memory pricing up 176% for DRAM and 100-120% for NAND in 2026, pushing operating margins to record levels. Goldman identifies seven stocks positioned to benefit from this supply crunch.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Goldman's top NAND-focused pick with expected meaningful earnings upward revisions due to tight supply and accelerating enterprise demand; 12-month price target of $700 implies 20% upside.
Kept at Neutral rating despite potential to capture ~20% HBM market share; Goldman would turn more constructive only with evidence of continued supply growth discipline into 2027.
Mentioned as memory-linked stock with upside potential in Goldman's report, benefiting from increased memory chip production demand.
Mentioned as memory-linked stock with upside potential in Goldman's report, expected to benefit from increased memory chip production.
